title: SQL Data Types Quiz
description: Test your fundamental knowledge about different data types in a relational database.

questions.question: Which SQL data type is essential for storing exact currency values without precision errors?
What's the primary difference between 'CHAR(n)' and 'VARCHAR(n)'?
options: CHAR is for numbers and VARCHAR is for letters.
CHAR is fixed-length (padded with spaces); VARCHAR is variable-length up to 'n'.
CHAR is faster to search than VARCHAR.

questions.question: Which data type should you use to store a user's long biography or blog post content?
What is 'TIMESTAMPTZ' in PostgreSQL?
options: A timestamp that is time-zone aware, ensuring correct time across different regions.
A way to automatically compress files.

questions.question: When should you use the 'JSONB' data type in a SQL database like PostgreSQL?
options: To store all of the application's data.
To store semi-structured or truly dynamic data that doesn't fit a fixed schema.
It's the only way to store data in a database.
